name: ALMA Gaussian Trend Pullback
edge_hypothesis: Inspired by Research Digest entry 'Gaussian Channel Trend Pullback' — Gaussian channel strategies perform well on crypto. ALMA (Arnaud Legoux Moving Average) uses a Gaussian-weighted window with offset, making it the closest available indicator to a Gaussian channel centerline. When price pulls back to ALMA and then crosses back above it (crosses_above ALMA) while the medium-term trend is confirmed bullish (close > EMA_50), this captures the exact moment a pullback ends and trend resumes. Unlike EMA crossovers (exhausted, 0 ACCEPTs), ALMA's Gaussian weighting responds differently to price patterns — it's smoother in trends and more responsive at reversals due to the offset parameter. The ALMA + EMA_50 combination has never been tested in this pullback-recovery configuration. Every existing spec using ALMA tested it as a crossover partner (T3 ALMA crossover), not as a pullback target.

variants.name: alma_pullback_ema50_v1_8to1
variants.description: ALMA as pullback target in EMA_50 trend. Price crossing back above ALMA after dipping below = pullback recovery. 8:1 R:R with 1.5x ATR stop. Tests whether ALMA's Gaussian weighting captures better pullback entries than standard MAs.
filters: EMA_50 trend gate: close above/below EMA_50
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted MA (9-period, offset 6, sigma 0.85). Pullback target. Price crossing back above ALMA = pullback recovery.
EMA_50 — medium-term trend direction filter. Only enter when price is on the trending side.

variants.name: alma_pullback_ema50_v2_10to1
variants.description: ALMA pullback with wider stop (2.0x ATR) and 10:1 R:R. The wider stop gives ALMA pullback more room for 4h noise. Tests optimal stop distance for Gaussian-weighted pullback entries.
filters: EMA_50 trend gate: close above/below EMA_50
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
R:R = 10:1. Needs 9.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted pullback target. Same signal as v1.
EMA_50 trend direction. Same as v1.

variants.name: alma_pullback_rsi_v3_8to1
variants.description: ALMA pullback with RSI confirmation instead of EMA_50 trend gate. RSI_14 > 40 confirms momentum is not deeply oversold (the pullback is healthy, not a reversal). This tests whether momentum confirmation (RSI) beats trend-direction confirmation (EMA_50) for ALMA pullback timing.
filters: RSI momentum band: 40-70 for longs, 30-60 for shorts
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted pullback target. Same cross signal.
RSI_14 between 40-70 (long) / 30-60 (short). Filters out entries at momentum extremes where pullback may be a reversal.

indicator_roles.confirmation: EMA_50 (trend direction, v1/v2) or RSI_14 (momentum band, v3)
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 for position sizing and R:R
